mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month increase in industrial production signals an expansion in aggregate demand that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in housing starts below 600,000 units signals a supply-side shock that compresses the S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in supply shock periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the unemployment rate above 9.5% signals a labor market shock that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in labor market shock periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the federal funds rate below 0.1% signals a monetary easing cycle that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real GDP above 13000 billion signals an expansion in aggregate demand that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production below 90 signals a supply-side shock that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in supply shock periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in initial unemployment claims above 600,000 signals a labor market shock that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in labor market shock periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real personal spending above 350 billion signals an expansion in aggregate demand that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
